# Benchmark: held-out v2 eval set

**Model**: claude-opus-4-8 · **Date**: 2026-06-02 · **Skill**: post-iteration-3
**Eval set**: `evals/evals-v2.json` — 24 brand-new prompts generated by a separate AI (see `evals/NEW-EVALS-PROMPT.md`), unseen by the skill author.

## Result

| Metric | Value |
|---|:--:|
| Evals | 24 |
| Assertions passed | **122 / 122** |
| Mean per-eval pass rate | **100%** |
| Evals below 1.0 | 0 |

Per-eval outputs + grading: [`../evals/results/v2/`](../evals/results/v2).

## Why this set matters

It's a held-out regression set that probes the **deliver-first vs. gate boundary in both directions** — the exact thing iteration-3 tuned:

- **gate_A** (#2 messaging hierarchy, #13 positioning, #14 value prop) → correctly **asked 2–3 questions first**.
- **deliver_first_trap** (#5 ICP, #17 content calendar) → correctly **delivered on stated assumptions, no gating**.
- **asset traps** → #15 (no asset) requested it; #16 hero + #8 checkout flow (provided inline) were **audited directly** without over-firing the "ask for the asset" gate.
- **honesty** (#19) → wrote the full supplement page with marked placeholders + health-claim caution, zero fabrication.
- **negative controls** (#22 resume, #23 TCP/UDP, #24 eulogy) → **did not trigger**.

## Caveats (read these)

- **1× run**, not 3×.
- **Self-graded**: produced and graded by `claude-opus-4-8` in one session, not an independent harness grader. Read 100% as *"no behavioral failures observed"* — not proof of ceiling-level copy quality. An independent grader (or a different model running the prompts) is the stronger check.
- **No baseline arm** — this set measures current-skill behavior/regressions, not a with-vs-without delta.
- Assertions were AI-authored, then sanity-checked for strictness before running.

## To harden this further

- Run the same 24 prompts on a **different model** and have a **third model grade**, to remove the author-grades-self bias.
- Promote v2 into the 3× harness alongside the original 26 for a combined regression suite.
